title: Shared dev key collapses all peers into one replay window - multi-node bench cannot converge
description:
ROOT CAUSE of the bench round-trip failures (2026-07-17). With CONFIG_LICHEN_L2_DEV_PROVISIONING, every node derives the SAME keypair from the shared dev_seed (lichen_l2.c:1052). LICHEN frames omit sender identity; attribution comes solely from which pinned peer pubkey verifies the Schnorr-48 signature (peer_try_all_pubkeys). With a shared key, ANY dev node's signed frame verifies as 'the' pinned peer, so ALL transmitters in RF range collapse into ONE replay window per receiver (commit_replay keys on auth->info.src_eui64 = pinned EUI).

Failure dynamics: link_ctx.c:83 draws a RANDOM epoch in [128,255] per boot (no persistence). Logical counter = (epoch<<16)|seq, half-space 8388607 > max epoch gap 127<<16, so the highest-epoch transmitter permanently claims the window and ALL other nodes are 100% replay-rejected (-120). The window retains the max-ever-accepted counter until the RECEIVER reboots, so rerolling transmitter epochs alone cannot recover. On the 3-node bench (T-Echo puck, T1000-E puck, Heltec V3 gateway) this made T-Echo<->gateway CoAP fail for hours while looking like RF or firmware trouble. Extra traps: successful RX logs at DBG only (accepts invisible on console), and any gateway console open reboots the ESP32 (bd 9ia2), rerolling its epoch and window.

VERIFIED WORKAROUND (round-trip completed 2026-07-17, three CoAP 2.05 responses with advancing gateway uptime): orchestrated reset sequence - (1) SMP-reset T1000-E if02, (2) SMP-reset T-Echo if04, (3) bounce gateway CP2102 port at +5s (auto-reset gives fresh window just before T-Echo's first GET at ~+11s). The T1000-E's ~15s post-reset TX quiet gap lets the T-Echo claim the gateway's fresh window and the gateway's response claim the T-Echo's fresh window, deterministic first-claim regardless of epoch draws. Script: scratchpad one_round.py (session 011WNEwnjQpVRfApJFak8Cpd).

REAL FIX options (feeds the real-provisioning work): (a) per-node dev keys derived from EUI-64 so attribution is genuine, (b) sender EUI-64 in the frame (design change), (c) dev-mode-only: reinit window on epoch mismatch (accepts cross-epoch replays; INSECURE-gated). Interim mitigation: keep the bench 2-node (power off extra pucks) - with 2 nodes first-claim always resolves correctly. Note also: T-Echo if04 SMP server wedged (CDC write timeout, app fine) after a reset dropped mid-SMP-transaction; recovered via 1200-baud DFU touch + UF2 reflash - datapoint for the CDC-ACM livelock bead.
close_reason: Fixed in 6354269f: per-node dev keys derived as SHA-512(dev_seed || EUI-64). lichen_link_derive_seed/derive_pubkey added to link_ctx; dev provisioning now pins the peer's DERIVED pubkey so signature verification attributes frames correctly and each peer gets its own replay window slot. Hardware-validated: T-Echo <-> V3 gateway sustain CoAP 2.05 round trips with the stale-shared-key T1000-E free-running (its frames now fail Schnorr verification, -200, instead of poisoning the window). The orchestrated reset workaround is obsolete. Note: all dev nodes must be reflashed together when crossing this commit - old/new dev firmwares cannot authenticate each other (T1000-E still on v1.0.1 is expected auth-fail noise until re-OTA'd). link_crypto 13/13, twister 47/47.
